prompt: I have a Magento 2 store running Hyvä 1.4 default theme on the main store view and Luma on a B2B store view. I just installed a third-party module (`Acme_Recommendations`) that only has Luma frontend support — it uses Knockout and a UI component. I need to make it work on the Hyvä side without breaking Luma. Walk me through creating a compatibility module.

assertions.name: uses-hyva-prefixed-handles
assertions.description: Recommends scoping all overrides to hyva_*-prefixed layout handles so Luma store view is unaffected

assertions.name: separate-module-not-theme-customization
assertions.description: Recommends a separate Magento module (composer package or app/code), not edits to the original module's vendor files

assertions.name: mentions-extra-hyva-themes-key
assertions.description: Mentions the `extra.hyva-themes.compat-module-for` Composer key (or hyva-themes.json) for registration

assertions.name: removes-knockout-and-ui-components
assertions.description: States that Knockout, UI components, RequireJS, and x-magento-init must be replaced (not retained) for the Hyvä side

assertions.name: uses-alpine-tailwind-replacement
assertions.description: Suggests Alpine.js + Tailwind for the Hyvä-side reimplementation

assertions.name: module-sequence-or-dependency
assertions.description: Recommends declaring a module sequence (etc/module.xml) or composer require dependency on the original module + Hyva_Theme

assertions.name: mentions-tracker-or-existing-compat
assertions.description: Mentions checking if a compat module already exists (compatibility module tracker) before building one — saves duplicate work
